candidates.page_title: MANNICH REACTION
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Mannich reaction describing aminomethylation of enolizable carbonyl compounds and related CH-acidic partners with formaldehyde and primary or secondary amines via iminium intermediates.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: iminium-ion formation from aldehydes and amines, attack of enolized carbonyl nucleophiles, utility of preformed iminium salts for milder conditions, and broad use of Mannich bases as synthetic intermediates.

candidates.page_title: Corey-Nicolaou Macrolactonization – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Corey-Nicolaou macrolactonization describing activation of hydroxy acids as 2-pyridinethiol esters using 2,2'-dipyridyl disulfide and triphenylphosphine, followed by intramolecular lactonization to medium- and large-ring lactones under high-dilution conditions.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: 2-pyridinethiol ester activation, dipolar intermediate, intramolecular lactonization, medium- and large-ring lactone formation.

candidates.page_title: Hajos-Parrish Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Hajos-Parrish reaction describing asymmetric intramolecular aldol cyclization of Michael adducts from 1,3-diones and enones under catalytic or stoichiometric amino-acid conditions to form bicyclic enones in high ee.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: proline-catalyzed Michael/aldol annulation, relation to the Wieland-Miescher ketone, amino-acid catalysis, dehydration to bicyclic enones, and mechanistic stereochemical models involving enamine or iminium activation.

candidates.page_title: Favorskii and Homo-Favorskii Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Favorskii and homo-Favorskii rearrangements describing skeletal rearrangement of alpha-halo ketones and ring-expanded or ring-contracted beta-halo ketones under basic conditions, often via cyclopropanone or cyclobutanone intermediates that are trapped by external nucleophiles.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: alpha-halo ketone rearrangement, cyclopropanone and cyclobutanone intermediates, regioselective ring opening, nucleophile trapping, and distinction between classical Favorskii, homo-Favorskii, and quasi-Favorskii pathways.

candidates.page_title: REGITZ DIAZO TRANSFER
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Regitz diazo transfer describing conversion of active methylene β-dicarbonyl compounds or α-formyl ketones into α-diazo carbonyl compounds by reaction with sulfonyl azides under basic conditions, including the deformylative diazo-transfer variant used to access α-diazo ketones.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: sulfonyl azides as diazo donors, requirement for sufficiently acidic methylene compounds or α-formyl ketones, triazene formation and fragmentation, and generation of versatile α-diazo carbonyl intermediates for Wolff rearrangement or metal-carbene chemistry.

candidates.page_title: Alder (Ene) Reaction (Hydro-Allyl Addition) – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_section_name: VII. Named Organic Reactions in Alphabetical Order
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the ene reaction, a pericyclic addition of an alkene bearing an allylic hydrogen to an enophile, with mechanistic comparison to the Diels–Alder reaction.
candidates.page_notes: Page explicitly mentions carbonyl-ene, aza-ene, imino-ene, hetero-ene, and metallo-ene variants together with a concerted six-membered transition state.

candidates.page_title: Amadori Reaction / Rearrangement – Importance, Mechanism, and Initial Application
candidates.page_section_name: VII. Named Organic Reactions in Alphabetical Order
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Amadori rearrangement describing conversion of N-glycosides to 1-amino-1-deoxyketoses under acid or Lewis-acid conditions, with mechanistic ring opening, enol formation, and tautomerization. Includes an initial synthetic application to D-fructose analogs.
candidates.page_notes: Page links the reaction to the Maillard reaction and non-enzymatic browning chemistry.

candidates.page_title: Aza-Claisen Rearrangement (3-Aza-Cope Rearrangement) – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_section_name: VII. Named Organic Reactions in Alphabetical Order
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the aza-Claisen rearrangement, the thermal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of N-allyl enamines, with comparison to neutral, charged, and keteneimine manifolds and a chairlike transition state.
candidates.page_notes: Also includes a synthetic application to (-)-isoiridomyrmecin from carbamide enolate rearrangement.

candidates.page_title: Claisen Condensation / Claisen Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Claisen condensation describing base-mediated ester enolate acylation to give beta-keto esters, including crossed Claisen condensation and Dieckmann cyclization variants.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: ester enolate generation, beta-keto ester formation, crossed Claisen and Dieckmann variants.

candidates.page_title: Cope Elimination / Cope Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Cope elimination describing oxidation of tertiary amines to N-oxides followed by stereospecific syn elimination upon heating to furnish alkenes and dialkylhydroxylamines through a five-membered cyclic transition state.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: tertiary amine N-oxide precursor, syn elimination, alkene formation, thermal beta-hydrogen removal.

candidates.page_title: Corey-Bakshi-Shibata Reduction (CBS Reduction) – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Corey-Bakshi-Shibata reduction describing enantioselective oxazaborolidine-catalyzed borane reduction of ketones to secondary alcohols through face-selective hydride delivery from a chiral catalyst-borane complex.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: oxazaborolidine catalyst, borane source, ketone-to-secondary-alcohol reduction, high enantioselectivity.

candidates.page_title: Corey-Chaykovsky Epoxidation and Cyclopropanation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Corey-Chaykovsky epoxidation and cyclopropanation describing base-generated sulfoxonium or sulfonium ylides that transfer methylene units to aldehydes, ketones, or alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl compounds to form epoxides or cyclopropanes.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: sulfur ylide generation with NaH in DMSO, carbonyl methylene transfer, epoxide versus cyclopropane formation.

candidates.page_title: Corey-Fuchs Alkyne Synthesis – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Corey-Fuchs alkyne synthesis describing one-carbon homologation of aldehydes to terminal alkynes through dibromoolefin formation with carbon tetrabromide and triphenylphosphine, followed by n-butyllithium-mediated conversion to lithium acetylides and hydrolysis.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: CBr4/Ph3P dibromoolefination, n-BuLi-induced elimination, aldehyde-to-terminal-alkyne homologation.

candidates.page_title: Corey-Kim Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Corey-Kim oxidation describing conversion of primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes or ketones with N-chlorosuccinimide, dimethyl sulfide, and triethylamine via alkoxysulfonium salt intermediates under mild low-temperature conditions.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: NCS/DMS activation, Corey-Kim reagent formation, alkoxysulfonium intermediates, mild alcohol oxidation.

candidates.page_title: Corey-Winter Olefination – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Corey-Winter olefination describing stereospecific conversion of 1,2-diols to alkenes through cyclic 1,3-dioxolane-2-thiones or 1,2-thionocarbonates followed by trialkyl phosphite-induced syn elimination.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: cyclic thionocarbonate formation, phosphite-promoted elimination, diol-to-alkene deoxygenation with stereospecific retention of alkene geometry.

candidates.page_title: Cornforth Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Cornforth rearrangement describing thermal isomerization of 4-carbonyl-substituted oxazoles through dicarbonylnitrile ylide intermediates to isomeric oxazole products, including mechanistic links to electrocyclic ring opening and 1,5-dipolar cycloreversion.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: oxazole isomerization, dicarbonylnitrile ylide intermediate, 4-carbonyl-substituted oxazole rearrangement, electrocyclic ring opening.

candidates.page_title: Criegee Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Criegee oxidation describing oxidative cleavage of vicinal diols to carbonyl compounds with lead tetraacetate under anhydrous organic conditions, including cyclic and acyclic substrates and mechanistic pathways involving lead glycolate intermediates.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: vicinal diol cleavage, Pb(OAc)4-mediated oxidation, lead glycolate intermediates, carbonyl-fragment generation from cyclic and acyclic 1,2-diols.

candidates.page_title: Curtius Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Curtius rearrangement describing thermal decomposition of acyl azides to isocyanates, which are then intercepted by water, amines, or alcohols to furnish amines, ureas, and carbamates. The page also compares routes from acid chlorides, mixed anhydrides, and carboxylic acids using azide-transfer reagents such as DPPA or trimethylsilyl azide.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: acyl azide formation, thermal loss of N2, isocyanate intermediates, amine/urea/carbamate product formation.

candidates.page_title: Dakin Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Dakin oxidation describing conversion of ortho- or para-hydroxylated, alkoxylated, or amino-substituted aromatic aldehydes and ketones to substituted phenols using hydrogen peroxide or organic peracids under basic conditions through O-acylphenol intermediates.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: hydroperoxide addition, 1,2-aryl shift, O-acylphenol intermediates, substituted phenol formation from activated aromatic carbonyl compounds.

candidates.page_title: Dakin-West Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Dakin-West reaction describing conversion of alpha-amino acids into alpha-acylamino ketones by treatment with acid anhydrides and base through mixed anhydride and oxazolone intermediates, followed by acyl transfer and decarboxylation.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: amino-acid N-acylation, mixed anhydride formation, oxazolone intermediates, alpha-acylamino ketone formation.

candidates.page_title: Danheiser Benzannulation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Danheiser benzannulation describing conversion of cyclobutenones or alpha-diazoketones and activated heterosubstituted alkynes into highly substituted aromatic rings through vinylketene generation, cycloaddition, electrocyclic ring opening, and dienylketene rearrangement.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: cyclobutenone annulation, vinylketene intermediates, [2+2] cycloaddition/electrocyclic cascade, highly substituted aromatic ring synthesis.

candidates.page_title: Danheiser Cyclopentene Annulation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Danheiser cyclopentene annulation describing TiCl4-promoted [3+2] annulation of trimethylsilylallenes with electron-deficient alkenes (allenophiles) to form highly substituted cyclopentenes through allylic cation capture and 1,2-silyl shift pathways.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: TiCl4 activation of enones, trimethylsilylallene addition, allylic carbocation intermediates, 1,2-silyl shift, highly substituted cyclopentene formation.

candidates.page_title: Danishefsky's Diene Cycloaddition – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for Danishefsky's diene cycloaddition describing the electron-rich 1-methoxy-3-trimethylsilyloxy-1,3-butadiene as a versatile heterodiene in Lewis-acid-mediated or thermal [4+2] cycloadditions, often proceeding through Mukaiyama-aldol or Diels–Alder manifolds to oxygenated cyclohexenones and dihydropyrones.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: Danishefsky diene, hetero Diels–Alder cycloaddition, Mukaiyama aldol pathway, Lewis-acid activation, oxygenated six-membered rings.

candidates.page_title: Darzens Glycidic Ester Condensation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Darzens glycidic ester condensation describing base-promoted reaction of aldehydes or ketones with alpha-halo esters, nitriles, sulfones, or amides to form glycidic esters or related epoxides through enolate addition and intramolecular SN2 ring closure.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: alpha-halo carbonyl pronucleophiles, Darzens condensation, halohydrin/enolate intermediates, glycidic ester formation, cis/trans epoxide control.

candidates.page_title: Davis' Oxaziridine Oxidations – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for Davis' oxaziridine oxidations describing N-sulfonyloxaziridines as neutral oxygen-transfer reagents for sulfide and selenide oxidation, amine N-oxidation, and especially alpha-hydroxylation of enolates to acyloins via SN2-type oxygen transfer.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: oxaziridine oxygen transfer, Davis reagent family, enolate alpha-hydroxylation, sulfide oxidation, non-chelated transition-state control.

candidates.page_title: De Mayo Cycloaddition (Enone-Alkene [2+2] Photocycloaddition) – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the De Mayo cycloaddition describing photochemical [2+2] cycloaddition between enones and alkenes to generate cyclobutanes or, via beta-hydroxy ketone intermediates and retro-aldol fragmentation, 1,5-diketones. The page emphasizes triplet-state exciplexes, 1,4-biradicals, and topology-dependent regioselectivity.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: triplet enone photochemistry, enone-alkene [2+2] cycloaddition, beta-hydroxy ketone intermediates, retro-aldol fragmentation, substituted cyclobutane formation.

candidates.page_title: Demjanov and Tiffeneau-Demjanov Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Demjanov and Tiffeneau-Demjanov rearrangements describing nitrous-acid-mediated ring expansion of aminomethylcycloalkanes or 1-aminomethylcycloalkanols to the corresponding cycloalkanol or cycloalkanone homologues through diazonium-ion formation and 1,2-alkyl migration.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: nitrosation to diazonium ions, ring expansion by 1,2-alkyl shift, Demjanov versus Tiffeneau-Demjanov substrate classes, competitive rearrangement pathways.

candidates.page_title: Dess-Martin Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Dess-Martin oxidation describing Dess-Martin periodinane (DMP), a soluble hypervalent iodine(V) reagent derived from IBX, as a mild and selective oxidant for 1° and 2° alcohols and oximes to the corresponding carbonyl compounds.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: IBX to DMP reagent preparation, hypervalent iodine oxidation, mild neutral conditions, aldehyde/ketone formation, alkoxyperiodinane intermediates.

candidates.page_title: Dieckmann Condensation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Dieckmann condensation describing intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters to cyclic beta-keto esters and the related Thorpe-Ziegler cyclization of dinitriles to enamino nitriles under strong base.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: intramolecular Claisen condensation, cyclic beta-keto ester formation, Thorpe-Ziegler cyclization, reversible enolate-driven ring closure, ring-size effects.

candidates.page_title: Dienone-Phenol Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the dienone-phenol rearrangement describing acid- or base-promoted migration of alkyl groups in cyclohexadienones to aromatic phenols, including ortho- and para-dienone cases and sequential 1,2-shift pathways that deliver highly substituted phenols.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: dienone rearomatization, alkyl migration, ortho versus para substitution patterns, 1,2-shift cascades, acid-catalyzed rearrangement of santonin-type frameworks.
